steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD and ZD using the specified drift equations.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period according to the procedure given in Section 7.
Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run (including the exact time of day) in the CEMS data log.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to the time and duration by first determining from the CEMS final output the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period.
Confirm that the pair of results are on a consistent moisture, temperature, and diluent concentration basis.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values, the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets, the mean of the differences, the standard deviation of each data set, and the CEMS RA.
Spike the appropriate metals in the feed or alter operation of the facility to raise the metal concentration to a level detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure.
Report CEMS measurements to the agency in units of µg/m3 on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.

preconditions.text: While the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.
If metals emissions concentrations from the source are so low as to be undetectable by the CEMS operating in its normal mode.

constraints.text: All CD determinations must be made following a 24-hour period during which no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place.
The CEMS zero point for each metal shall not drift by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for that metal.
The CEMS calibration may not drift or deviate from the reference value of the calibration standard used for each metal by more than 5 percent of the emission standard.
